problem: Given that $13^{-1} \equiv 29 \pmod{47}$, find $34^{-1} \pmod{47}$, as a residue modulo 47.  (Give a number between 0 and 46, inclusive.)

classified_questions.question: What is the definition of finding the inverse of a number modulo some value?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we use the given congruence to find the inverse of 34 modulo 47?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: Can we rewrite 34 as an equivalent congruence that we can work with more easily?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we express (-13)^{-1} in terms of 13^{-1}?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the relationship between (-1)^{-1} and -1 when working with modular arithmetic?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: How can we calculate the value of (-1) \cdot 29 as a residue modulo 47?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What is the final result of finding 34^{-1} \pmod{47} as a residue modulo 47?
classified_questions.label: b
